description: Utility-first lawn care app with zip-to-zone onboarding, grass type identification quiz, university extension PDF parsing, and personalized care plans with dark/green PWA theme

userStories.title: Scaffold Next.js 15 project
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the base project scaffolded so all other stories have a working foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize project with `npx create-next-app@latest` using App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ESLint, and src/ directory structure
Remove default Next.js placeholder content from app/page.tsx and app/globals.css
Install core dependencies: @clerk/nextjs, @vercel/postgres, ai, @anthropic-ai/sdk, next-pwa
package.json scripts include dev, build, start, and lint
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Global dark/green theme and layout shell
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the global design tokens and layout shell so every page shares the correct dark/green visual theme.
acceptanceCriteria: tailwind.config.ts defines custom colors: background #0d1a0d, surface #1a2e1a, primary #4caf50, secondary #a5d6a7, text-primary #e8f5e9, text-muted #81c784
app/globals.css applies background and text-primary as default body styles
app/layout.tsx renders a full-height dark layout with a top navigation bar showing the app name 'LawnGuide' in green
No light mode styles exist anywhere in the codebase
All text on dark backgrounds passes WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1) — verify visually
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Database schema: user_profiles and cached_plans tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables for storing user lawn profiles and cached extension plan data so the app can persist and retrieve user data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file at db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql

user_profiles table columns: id (uuid pk), clerk_user_id (text unique nullable), session_id (text unique nullable), zip_code (text), state (text), usda_zone (text), grass_type (text), square_footage (integer nullable), has_pets (boolean default false), sun_exposure (text), created_at (timestamptz default now), updated_at (timestamptz default now)
cached_plans table columns: id (uuid pk), state (text), grass_type (text), pdf_url (text), parsed_plan (jsonb), created_at (timestamptz default now)
Unique constraint on cached_plans(state, grass_type)
Migration runs successfully against the connected Postgres database
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Clerk authentication setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Clerk auth wired into the app so users can optionally create accounts to save their profile across devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and configure @clerk/nextjs with NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and CLERK_SECRET_KEY env vars (use placeholder values in .env.local.example)
src/middleware.ts uses Clerk's clerkMiddleware() to protect only /dashboard and /profile routes; onboarding routes remain public
app/layout.tsx wraps children in <ClerkProvider>
Clerk's <SignInButton> and <UserButton> are conditionally shown in the nav bar (SignInButton when signed out, UserButton when signed in)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ZIP code to USDA hardiness zone API route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API route that resolves a ZIP code to a USDA hardiness zone and state so the onboarding flow can personalize the plan.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/zip-zone-data.ts containing a static lookup map of ZIP code prefixes (first 3 digits) to { state: string, zone: string } — cover all 50 US states with representative zone values
Create app/api/zone/route.ts that accepts GET ?zip=XXXXX, looks up the zone, and returns { zip, state, zone } as JSON
Returns 400 with { error: 'Invalid ZIP code' } for non-5-digit input
Returns 404 with { error: 'Zone not found for this ZIP' } if no match
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Grass types static data and quiz questions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need static data files for grass types and quiz questions so the identification flow has content to work from.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/data/grass-types.ts exporting an array of GrassType objects each with: id, name, description, bladeWidth ('fine'|'medium'|'coarse'), texture ('soft'|'rough'|'stiff'), color ('bright-green'|'dark-green'|'blue-green'), growthPattern ('clumping'|'spreading'), activeSeason ('warm'|'cool'|'year-round'), photoUrl (placeholder string), traits (string[])
Include at least 10 grass types: Bermudagrass, Zoysia, St. Augustine, Centipede, Bahia, Kentucky Bluegrass, Tall Fescue, Fine Fescue, Perennial Ryegrass, Buffalo Grass
Create src/data/quiz-questions.ts exporting an array of 5 QuizQuestion objects each with: id, question (string), options (array of { label, value, trait, traitValue })
Quiz questions cover: blade width, texture, color, growth pattern, active season
Create src/lib/grass-scorer.ts with a scoreGrassTypes(answers: Record<string, string>) function that returns grass types ranked by how many quiz answers match each type's traits
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: University extension PDF discovery API route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend route that finds the most relevant university extension PDF URL for a given US state and grass type.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/data/extension-sources.ts with a static map of state abbreviation to { universityName: string, baseUrl: string, searchUrl: string } for all 50 states (use land-grant universities: e.g. NC State, UGA, Texas A&M, Rutgers, etc.)
Create app/api/find-pdf/route.ts accepting POST { state, grassType }
Route constructs a targeted search query combining state extension URL + grass type keyword
Route uses fetch to search the extension site or falls back to a known PDF URL pattern per state
Returns { pdfUrl: string, source: string } on success or { error: string } with fallback flag
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PDF fetch and Claude AI parse to structured monthly plan
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend route that fetches a PDF from a URL and uses the Claude API to extract a structured month-by-month care plan.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/api/parse-pdf/route.ts accepting POST { pdfUrl, state, grassType }
Route fetches the PDF as a buffer using node fetch

Route calls the Anthropic Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-6) with the PDF content and a system prompt instructing it to extract a JSON array of { month: number, tasks: [{ title, description, category, priority, petSafetyNote }] }
Valid categories: 'mow' | 'fertilize' | 'water' | 'aerate' | 'seed' | 'pest-weed' | 'other'
Valid priorities: 'urgent' | 'routine' | 'optional'
Returns the parsed plan JSON on success
Saves the result to the cached_plans table using state + grassType as the cache key
If a cached plan already exists for that state + grassType, returns the cached version without re-parsing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: User profile server actions (save and retrieve)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need server actions for saving and loading a user's lawn profile so the frontend can persist data across the onboarding steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/actions/profile.ts with server actions: saveProfile(data: ProfileData) and getProfile(sessionId: string)
saveProfile upserts a user_profiles row using session_id as the key; if the user is signed in via Clerk, also sets clerk_user_id
getProfile retrieves the profile by session_id OR clerk_user_id (whichever is available)
ProfileData type includes all user_profiles fields except id and timestamps
Session ID is a UUID generated client-side on first visit and stored in localStorage under the key 'lawn_session_id'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Onboarding wizard shell with progress indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want a step-by-step onboarding flow with a clear progress indicator so I always know where I am in the setup process.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/onboarding/layout.tsx rendering a centered single-column container (max-w-md) on the dark background
Progress indicator at the top shows 5 steps as dots/segments; active step is green (#4caf50), completed steps are dimmed green, future steps are dark
Step routing uses app/onboarding/[step]/page.tsx where step is 1 through 5
Navigating to /onboarding redirects to /onboarding/1
A 'Back' button is shown on steps 2–5 and navigates to the previous step
Onboarding state (all collected answers so far) is persisted in sessionStorage under 'onboarding_state' between steps
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Onboarding Step 1: ZIP code entry
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to enter my ZIP code on the first screen so the app can determine my growing zone.
acceptanceCriteria: app/onboarding/1/page.tsx renders a full-screen step with headline 'Where is your lawn?' and a ZIP code input field
Input accepts only 5 numeric digits
On submit, calls GET /api/zone?zip=XXXXX and shows a loading state
On success, displays the resolved zone (e.g. 'Zone 7a — North Carolina') in green text below the input
A 'Continue' button appears after successful zone resolution and advances to step 2
Error state shows 'We couldn't find a zone for that ZIP. Please check and try again.' in red
Resolved zip, state, and zone are saved to sessionStorage onboarding_state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Onboarding Step 2: Yard profile inputs
userStories.description: As a user, I want to enter my yard details so the plan can give me accurate task quantities and relevant advice.
acceptanceCriteria: app/onboarding/2/page.tsx renders headline 'Tell us about your yard'
Square footage field: numeric input with label 'Approximate lawn size (sq ft)' and a 'Not sure' toggle that sets value to null and shows 'We'll use a standard yard size'
Pets toggle: labeled 'Do you have pets or animals that use the lawn?' with Yes/No buttons
Sun exposure: three card-style buttons for 'Full Sun', 'Partial Shade', 'Full Shade' — selected card has green border
'Continue' button is enabled only after pets and sun exposure are selected (sq ft is optional)
All values saved to onboarding_state in sessionStorage on Continue
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Onboarding Step 3: Grass identification quiz
userStories.description: As a user, I want to answer questions about my grass one at a time so the app can suggest what grass type I have.
acceptanceCriteria: app/onboarding/3/page.tsx shows one quiz question at a time from src/data/quiz-questions.ts
Each question shows 3–4 answer options as large tap-friendly cards with label and optional icon
Selecting an answer immediately advances to the next question (no separate Next button)
Progress within the quiz is shown as 'Question 2 of 5' text
A 'Skip quiz — I know my grass type' link at the bottom navigates directly to step 4 with no quiz answers
After the last question, calls scoreGrassTypes() with the collected answers and saves results + answers to onboarding_state, then navigates to step 4
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Onboarding Step 4: Grass type photo confirmation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see photos of the suggested grass types so I can confirm which one is in my yard.
acceptanceCriteria: app/onboarding/4/page.tsx renders headline 'Does your grass look like this?'
Shows top 2–3 ranked grass types from onboarding_state (or full list if quiz was skipped)
Each grass type card shows: photo (use photoUrl from grass-types data, placeholder image if empty), grass name, and 3 key trait chips (e.g. 'Fine blade · Spreads · Warm season')
Tapping a card selects it (green border) and shows a 'This is my grass' confirm button
A 'None of these match' link at the bottom shows all 10+ grass types in a scrollable list
Confirmed grass type saved to onboarding_state and advances to step 5
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Onboarding Step 5: Save profile and trigger plan generation
userStories.description: As a user completing onboarding, I want my profile saved and my care plan generated while I optionally create an account.
acceptanceCriteria: app/onboarding/5/page.tsx renders headline 'Your plan is being built...' with an animated green loading spinner
On mount, calls saveProfile() server action with all onboarding_state data using a generated session UUID stored in localStorage
Simultaneously, calls POST /api/find-pdf then POST /api/parse-pdf in sequence to generate and cache the user's plan
While loading, shows encouraging copy: 'We're finding your local university extension guide and building your personalized plan'
On completion, shows optional account creation: 'Save your plan across devices — create a free account' with Sign Up and 'Continue without account' options
Both paths navigate to /dashboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 'Do This Now' dashboard — current week tasks
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see what lawn tasks I should do this week so I can take immediate action.
acceptanceCriteria: app/dashboard/page.tsx loads the user's profile via getProfile() and their cached plan from cached_plans
Filters the plan to the current month's tasks
Renders a 'Right Now' section at the top showing tasks for the current week, sorted by priority (urgent first)
Each task card shows: category icon, task title, 1–2 sentence description, priority badge (red=urgent, yellow=routine, gray=optional)
If has_pets is true and a task has a petSafetyNote, show a yellow 'Pet caution' badge with the note in a tooltip/expandable
Tasks involving fertilizer or product quantities show amounts scaled to the user's square footage (e.g. '2.5 lbs nitrogen per 1,000 sq ft × your 3,200 sq ft = 8 lbs total')
Empty state: 'Nothing urgent this week — check back soon or view your full annual calendar'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Annual care calendar view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all 12 months of lawn care tasks in a calendar so I can plan ahead for the whole year.
acceptanceCriteria: app/dashboard/calendar/page.tsx renders a full annual calendar below a 'Your Year at a Glance' heading
12 month columns displayed horizontally with horizontal scroll on mobile
Current month is highlighted with a green border
Each month shows category color chips for tasks present that month (mow=green, fertilize=yellow, water=blue, aerate=orange, seed=lime, pest-weed=red, other=gray)
Tapping a month expands it (accordion or modal on mobile) to show the full task list for that month
Expanded task list matches the same card design as the 'Do This Now' view
A toggle at the top switches between 'Calendar View' and 'List View' (list view shows all months stacked vertically)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Sign in / sign up pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want dedicated sign-in and sign-up pages so I can create or access my account at any time.
acceptanceCriteria: app/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx renders Clerk's <SignIn> component centered on the dark background with green accent styling
app/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx renders Clerk's <SignUp> component with the same styling
Both pages have the LawnGuide logo/name at the top
After sign-in, redirects to /dashboard
After sign-up, redirects to /dashboard (profile from localStorage session is merged with the new Clerk account via clerk_user_id update)
Clerk appearance config matches dark theme (background, text, button colors)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Profile edit page
userStories.description: As a returning user, I want to edit my yard profile so my plan stays accurate if things change.
acceptanceCriteria: app/profile/page.tsx is protected by Clerk middleware (redirects to sign-in if not authenticated)
Page shows a form pre-populated with current profile values from getProfile()
All onboarding fields are editable: ZIP code, grass type (dropdown from grass-types data), square footage, pets toggle, sun exposure
Changing the grass type or ZIP code shows a warning: 'Changing these fields will regenerate your plan'
Save button calls saveProfile() server action and re-triggers plan generation if grass type or zip changed
Success toast: 'Profile updated' in green
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PWA manifest and service worker
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to install the app to my home screen and access my plan offline.
acceptanceCriteria: public/manifest.json defines: name 'LawnGuide', short_name 'LawnGuide', theme_color '#0d1a0d', background_color '#0d1a0d', display 'standalone', start_url '/', icons at 192x192 and 512x512 (green leaf icon on dark background)
next.config.ts configures next-pwa with dest: 'public', disable in development, and caches the /dashboard page and its API responses
app/layout.tsx links to the manifest and sets theme-color meta tag
Lighthouse PWA audit passes (run via Chrome DevTools or Lighthouse CLI) — score 90+
Typecheck passes
